title: Experimental determination of the 7-Be+p scattering lengths

lengths

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.A, Vol.454, p.350 (2000)

x4freetext: LEDA Detector
system.
RIB facility
The target consisted of a thin polyethylene
self-supporting foil with a very thin Au coating
evaporated on its upstream face. The Au layer was
used for normalization. The effect of this Au layer
on the beam energy loss was negligible.
Two PIPS (passivated implanted planar silicon)
detectors located at 60 degrees.
The recoil protons were detected in two LEDA
4 multi-strip systems located at 12.6 and 61.7 cm from
the target. A 12 um thick Mylar foil was positioned
in front of all LEDA sectors to stop the high energy
scattered 7Be and the recoil 12C particles for the
beam energies of 6.2 and 5.6 MeV.
Be-7 beam has been used.
From a R-matrix analysis of the cross
section data, the energy and the width of the 1+
resonance (Ex = 0.77 MeV) have been obtained. The
l = 0 scattering lengths a/01 = 25+/-9 fm (channel
spin I = 1) and a/02 = -7+/-3 fm (channel spin I = 2)
have been deduced. Implications on the low energy
S-factor of the 7Be(p,gamma) 8B reaction are discussed.
